So, we started Jalen’s Gift Foundation to help other families going through a loss, to provide multiple free services, such as photograph, grief support and help towards memorial services.” ***Download the Born too soon media resources
translations.hypothesis: Despite many positive changes that have been made in recent years, we still need to address the issue of preterm birth, which remains a major cause of infant mortality worldwide. This is a complex issue, and it requires a multi-faceted approach. It is time to invest in better care for preterm babies and their mothers, and to ensure that all children have the opportunity to reach their full potential. Helga Fogstad, Director of PMNCH (Partnership for Maternal, Newborn, and Child Health) Preterm birth refers to the birth of a baby before 37 weeks of gestation. It is a leading cause of infant mortality and morbidity worldwide. Improving the care of preterm babies and their mothers, and ensuring safe delivery practices, can save lives and improve the health and well-being of families. However, progress has been hampered by a number of factors, including the COVID-19 pandemic, climate change, conflict, and rising costs of healthcare. By working together – governments, healthcare providers, the private sector, civil society, parents, and educators – we can overcome these challenges and create a world where all babies have the opportunity to be born healthy and thrive. Gabriela and Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, United States: The parents of Jalen Foster, founder of the Jalen Gift Foundation "Our foundation was established after the death of our son, Jalen Foster, who was born prematurely at 27 weeks due to an unknown infection. The loss of our son was devastating, and we felt alone and unsupported. We wanted to help other families who have gone through the same experience. That's why we started the Jalen Gift Foundation, to provide free resources and support to families affected by preterm birth, such as baby supplies, counseling, and educational materials."

translations.source: I-UEA neWHO ihambise iziphakamisi moya zamasaplayi ezokwelapha ezibucayi eSudan United Arab Emirates (UAE) neNhlangano yeZempilo eMhlabeni (WHO) bahambise amathoni angu-30 amasaplayi ezokwelapha okuphuthumayo eSudan namhlanje. Ibhanoyi eliphethe amasaplayi yokwelapha abalimele, ukuhlinza okuphuthumayokanye nezidakamizwa eziphuthumayo lifike isikhumulweni samabhanoyi iPort Sudan ekuseni kakhulu. Ukuhanjswa kwenani elingu US$ 444 000, eyokuqala iWHO ekwazile ukuyihambisa ngezomoya iya eSudan kusukela kube nokubheduka kwengxabano. I-WHO ihambise amasaplayi ezindaweni zempilo ngaphambi kokunyuka kwengxabano. Babekhathele ngemva kwezinsuku ezimbalwa uma ubheka inamba yabalimele. Ukukhishwa kwendizamshini kuza njengengxenye ye-UAE imizamo yokuqhubeka nokukhulula ekwesekeni abantu baseSudanese nokuveza ubuhlobo obunzulu neSudan, ikakhulukazi ngokwesimo samanje esibhekene nezwe. Ukukhululwa kuphinde kube ukululwa kobuntu be-UAE umbono nesikalo sobudlelwano namanye amazwe ancike kubantu nouzibophezela kokusiza abanye ngezikhathi zezinkinga nezimo eziphuthumayo. UDkt Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Unqongqoshe wezwe leInternational Cooperation, uthe i-UAE izoqhubeka nokusebenzisana neNhlangano yezempilo emhlabeni ukuqinisekisa ukubekwa okuyimpumelelo kokusebenza kwelojistikhi ebucayi nokuthumela ezokwelapha ngokuphuthuma nosizo lokudla ukusiza ukubhekana nenkinga yamanje eSudan. Nokuhambisana nesimemezelo kuzona zonke izinngxenye zengxabano yamanje ukuyeka manje unya nokuqhuba isimo esidala ukuhlupheka okungashiwo kubantu baseSudan, i-UAE isheshile ekuzibophezelei kwayo ukunikeza usizo nokusiza mazwe ngesikhathi beludinga. I-UAE igxile kakhulu ekunikezeni amaqoqo abuthaka kakhulu akhahlamezekile kulesisimo saseSudan, ikakhulukazi abagulayo, izingane,nabadala nabesifazane abasengcupheni yokuqhubeka kwengxabano - futhi lamabhanoyi akhululayo azobhekana ngqo nezikhala eziphuthumayo kwezokulapha kanye nokuhlinzekwa ngokudla. I-UAE igcine ngokujula ubuntu kusho ukuthi isiqinisekisile iyaqhubeka nokuxhumana nokumhlaba okukhulu nokuzinikela kokuqinisa ukuthula, ukuphepha nozinzo, ayi esizindeni kuphela kodwa emhlabeni wonke ngesikhathi nababambisani nomphakathi wamazwe angaphandle, izwe liyaqhubeka nokusiza iSudanese ezikhathini ezinzima. Amasaplayi ezempiloafaka ukuthuka okwanele, amasaplayi okuhlinza okuphuthumayo nemithi ebalulekile ukuthi ifike manje kubantu abangu-165 000 abadinga kakhulu usizo lobuntu. Izindawo zezempilo ezweni lonke zibika ukuthi seziphelelwe masaplayi ayisisekelo sezempilo nokuthi izitolo zokwelapha zezwe akusafinyeleki kuzona ngenxa yezokuphepha ezweni. Kokunye ukwesekwa, iWHO izobeka amalojistikhi amabili okuphuthumayo ukuhamba ngebhanoyi ukuqinisekisa lamasaplayi asehanjiswa ezindaweni zempilo ezinkulu ezingu-13 ukweseka abasebenzi bezempilo nokwengeza ukunakekela kulabo abakudingayo. WHO inamathoni amakhulu angu-30 amasaplayi amalalwveva nezifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo njengehayihayi noshukela, izimo ezingabulala uma zishiywa zingalashiwe. Lokhu nezikhwama zegazi ezingu-23 000 sezilungisiwe nelojistikhi yomhlaba yeWHO uhabhu kuInternational Humanitarian City neWHO njengamanje ibheka zonke izindlela zokuletha lamasaplayi eSudan ngokushesha ekubambisaneni noNgqongqoshe weZempilo eSudan. UDkt Michael Ryan, uMqondisi omkhulu wohlelo lweziphuthumayo zempilo weWHO, uthe Iwho Global Logistic Hub e-UAE isibe okubalulekile kokuphindeka kwempendulo yesaplayi esigamekweni ezibucayi emhlabeni. Ukweseka amazwe angaphezu kuka-140 kuzona zonke izindawo zezifunda zweWHO, ihabhu inikezela ngempilo yonke yeMember States kusipiliyoni sokuphuthumayo kwezempilo okuvelayo ekubhedukeni kwezifo, izinhlekelele njengezikhukhula nokuzamazama komhlaba, nomthelela wengxabano ezinhlelweni zempilo. Ukwesekwa okunikeziwe yi-UAE ivumele iWHO ukuthi ibe phambili ekuphenduleni kweziphuthumayo zempilo-ukuhambisa usizo longoti bezokwelapha kulabo abadingayo lapho kudingeka khona. Njenge ngxenye yokuqinisa ezokwakha umhlaba kwezokulungiselela eziphuthumayo zempilo, ukuphendula nokubekezela Iuae, iWHO nokubambisana bayasebenzisana ukuhambisa amasaplayi ukufinyelela kwababuthaka kakhulu emhlabeni esikhathini sabo abadinga usizo ngayo. Ngengxenye yakhe, uDkt Ahmed Al-Mandhari, uMqondisi wesifunda seWHO wase-Eastern Mediterranean, uthe iNhlangano yeZempilo eMhlabeni izoqhubeka nokuxhumanisa nabasemagunyeni bezempilo eSudan ukuletha amasaplayi kulabo ababuthaka kakhulu emhlabeni esikhathi sokudinga usizo kakhulu. Ngenxa yokuxabana, izibhedlela, amakhemisi nezindawo zokunakekela ezempilo yokuqala seziphelelwe okubalulekile, okuyimithi yokusindisa izimpilo. Ukufika kwamathani amakhulu angeziwe awu-30 okukhathazeka namasaplayi ezokuhlinza okuphuthumayo kanye nemithi ebalulekile ukukwazi iWHO izokweseka izindawo zempilo ezinkulu ezingu-13 nokwenza kabusha kwezempilo umsebenzi wokunakekela kulabo abadingayo. Ilojistikhi yomhhlaba wethu kuhabhu yaseDubai idlala indawo ebaluleke kakhulu ukuqinisekisa ukungaphazamiseki kwesaplayi kwezempilo futhi siyajabula ukwesekwa esikunikiwe yi-United Arab Emirates ukusiza ishatha yokuqala bebhanoyi lalamasaplayi adingeka ngokushesha.
translations.reference: UAE and WHO deliver air lift of critical medical supplies to Sudan The United Arab Emirates (UAE) and the World Health Organization have delivered 30 tons of urgent medical supplies to Sudan today. A plane carrying supplies for injury treatment, emergency surgeries, and essential drugs arrived in Port Sudan Airport early this morning. The shipment, valued at US$ 444 000, is the first that WHO has been able to deliver by air to Sudan since the outbreak of the conflict. WHO distributed supplies to heath facilities prior to the escalation of conflict. These were exhausted after a few days given the number of injured. The dispatch of the aircraft comes as part of the UAE's continuous relief efforts in support of the Sudanese people and reflects its deep relations with Sudan, particularly in light of the current situation the country is facing. The relief is also an extension of the UAE’s humanitarian vision and underscores its relations with other countries based on human fraternity and its commitment to assisting others during crises and emergency situations. Dr Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Minister of State for International Cooperation, said that the UAE "continues to work closely alongside the World Health Organization to ensure the successful deployment of the critical logistics operations to send urgent medical and food aid to help address the current crisis in Sudan. In parallel with calls for all sides of the current conflict to immediately cease hostilities and de-escalate a situation that is causing untold suffering for the Sudanese people, the UAE is steadfast in its commitment to providing aid and assistance to countries in times of need. The UAE is particularly focused on providing for the most vulnerable groups affected by the situation in Sudan, especially the sick, children, the elderly, and women who are most at risk from the ongoing concerning conflict – and these relief flights will directly address the most pressing gaps in medical and food provision. The UAE's deeply held humanitarian values have meant that it has ensured it has continued to communicate to the world its robust and unrelenting dedication to strengthening peace, security and stability, not just regionally, but also worldwide, whilst alongside its partners and the international community, the country continues to assist the Sudanese people in times of crisis.” The health supplies include enough trauma, emergency surgical supplies, and essential medicines to immediately reach 165 000 people who are in desperate need of humanitarian aid. Health facilities across the country report they have run out of basic health supplies and that national medical stores are no longer accessible due to the security situation. Among other support, WHO is deploying two emergency logisticians travelling with the cargo to ensure that these supplies are immediately distributed to 13 major health facilities to support healthcare workers and extend care to those in need. WHO has another 30 metric tons of supplies for malaria and noncommunicable diseases like diabetes and hypertension, conditions that can become deadly if left untreated. These and about 23 000 blood bags are being readied within the WHO global logistics hub in the International Humanitarian City, and WHO is currently exploring all possibilities for delivering these supplies to Sudan as quickly as possible in collaboration with the Sudanese Ministry of Health. Dr Michael Ryan, Executive Director, of the WHO Health Emergencies Programme, said, “The WHO Global Logistics Hub in the UAE has become a centerpiece of rapid supply response to acute events globally. Supporting over 140 countries across all 6 WHO geographic regions, the hub provides a lifeline to Member States experiencing health emergencies arising from outbreaks of diseases, disasters such as floods and earthquakes, and the impact of conflict on health systems. The support provided by the UAE enables WHO to be at the forefront of these health emergency responses – delivering specialist medical aid to those in need wherever and whenever it is needed. As part of efforts to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience the UAE, WHO, and partners are working hand-in-hand to deliver supplies to reach the world’s most vulnerable in their time of greatest need.” For his part, Dr Ahmed Al-Mandhari, WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean, said, “The World Health Organization continues to coordinate with the Sudanese health authorities to deliver essential health supplies through all available pathways. Due to the conflict, hospitals, pharmacies and primary health care facilities have stocked out of essential, life-saving medicines. The arrival of an additional 30 metric tons of trauma and emergency surgery supplies as well as essential medicines will enable WHO to support 13 major health facilities and re-establish health care services for those in need. Our global logistics hub in Dubai plays a vital role in ensuring an uninterrupted supply of health commodities and we are grateful for the support provided by the United Arab Emirates to facilitate our first charter flight of these desperately needed supplies.
translations.hypothesis: The UAE and WHO have jointly dispatched 30 containers of essential medical supplies to Sudan. The shipment, valued at $444,000, is the first of several planned deliveries to the country. The supplies include essential medicines, medical equipment, and personal protective equipment. The UAE's decision to provide aid comes after several weeks of intense fighting. The UAE has also pledged to provide humanitarian assistance to Sudanese refugees. Dr. Reem Al Hashimy, the UAE's Minister of International Cooperation, said that the UAE is committed to providing humanitarian assistance to Sudan and other countries in need. The UAE is also working to provide support to Sudanese refugees in neighboring countries. The WHO has also dispatched medical supplies to Sudan, as well as to other countries in the region. The supplies include essential medicines, medical equipment, and personal protective equipment. The WHO is also working to provide training to Sudanese healthcare workers. The WHO has also launched a global logistics hub in the UAE to help distribute medical supplies to countries in need. The hub is expected to play a key role in the response to the ongoing crisis in Sudan.

translations.source: Kungukweseka okunjalo njengoba lokhu kukhombisa ukwenza kombono wethu eSifundeni wezempilo kuwona wonke umuntu yiwona wonke umuntu. Usizo lwezokwelapha olunikezwe Inhlangano yezempilo emhlabeni nge-UAE kubalelwe kwafinyelela ku-55% isamba samasaplayi okwelapha anikezwe iSudan asuka phesheya ngo-2022, ukuveza isikhundla seUAE njengehabhu enkulu yosizo loluntu emazweni angaphandle.
translations.reference: It is support such as this that shows in action our Regional vision of health for all, by all.” The medical aid provided by the World Health Organization through the UAE accounted for approximately 55% of the total medical supplies provided to Sudan from abroad in 2022, reflecting the UAE's position as a major hub for international humanitarian assistance.
translations.hypothesis: This highlights the importance of healthcare access for all individuals, regardless of their location. The World Health Organization (WHO) estimates that 55% of healthcare needs in Sudan were met in 2022, demonstrating the UAE's role as a major provider of humanitarian aid to other countries.

translations.source: Ukubambisana endaweni kubalulekile ekuqhubekeni emhlabeni okuya kokuhlobene nezempilo zamaSDG Umbiko wokuqhubeka omusha ukhishwe ngokuhlanganyela namhlanje kuma ejensi asayinile angu-13 eGlobal Action Plan for Helath Lives and Well being for All (SDG3 GAP). Umbiko usihloko esthi "What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?" uma kubhekwa iminyaka emine kusetshenzwa ndawonye ngama-ejensi ezindlela eziningi ezinkulu kuvezwa ukuthi yikuphi okusebenzile nokungasebenzanga ekuqinisweni ukubambisana nokwesekwa kwenqubeko yezwe okuuya nokuhlobene nezempilo zeSustainable Development Goals (amaSDG). Lo nyaka umaka iphuzu lahhafu eliya kumaSDG kodwa umhlaba usele ngemuva ukufika kuzinhloso zomhlaba. Kusukela ngokwethulwa kwayo ngo-2019, iSDG3 GAP yenze ukubambisana okusha kwezinhlaka phakathi kokusayina kwama ejensi ezindaweni ezibalulekile njengokugcineka kwezimali nokunakekelwa kwezempilo kokuqala kwabanye abantu. Okungenani amazwe angu-67 akhulumile kanye noma ngaphezulu ngeSDG3 GAP ukuqhuba izingqikithi. Senze inqubeko ebalulekile, kodwa kusanendlela ende okumele siyihambe ukwenza ngcono indlela izinhlangano eziningi ezisebenzisana ngayo ukweseka amazwe. Kumele silalele esikutshelwa ngamazwe futhi silandele imiyalelo,kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO noSihlalo weSDG3 GAP kuzinhloko zeqoqo. "Ngibonga ababambisani ngokubambisana kwazo nokuzihlola ngokwethembeka okuqukethwe kulombiko. "Umbiko unikeza iziphakamiso eziyisithupha ezibalulekile ukuqinisekisa ukuthi amazwe ayazuza ekwesekweni okukhishiwe nokubona ukuzibophezela okwenziwe kuSDG3 GAP. Lokhu kufaka: Ukuqhubeka kokwenza ngcono kweSDG3 GAP kwezempilo ngokufuna imibono yeMember State ukuthi sibambisana kanjani ezingeni lezwe nokuphendula okuhlobene neziphakamiso; Ukugcina iGAP njengenkundla yokubambisana; ukulondoloza ukubambisana okuqinile ezingeni lezwe ekunakekelweni kwezempilo kokuqala nokubheka izindlela ezintsha njengesimo sezulu sokubekezela kuzinhlelo zempilo; Ngokuhlanganyela sebenzisa izindlela ezintsha ezingeni lezwe njengokuhambisa indlela yomthelela; ukukhuluma kakhulu nomphakathi; kanye nokusebenza neMember States ukuqinisa imiklomelo yokubambisana ngobuholi bezepolitiki,indlela yokubusa noxhasomali lokweseka ukubambisana. Imiphumela ebalulekile yombiko sizoyifaka ezingxoxweni kuUnited Nations General Assembly SDG Summit elandelayo nomhlangano wezinga eliphezulu kuUniversal Helath Coverage ngoMandulo 2023. Izinyanga eziholela kulezigameko ezibalulekile zinikeza ithuba ukuphusha ndawonye yiMember States kamaejensi ezindlela eziningi ukuhlonza izindlela ukuthi kuzokwenziwa kanjani iziphakamiso ukulungiselella indawo yesibambiso esiphelele ukuqina kokubambisana nokuqhuba inqubeko eya kokuhlobene nezempilo SDG kuhhafu wesibili weSDG wesikhathi esibekiwe. Inothi eliya kumhleli: I-SDG3 GAPichaza ukuthi ukusayina kwama ejensi angu-13 kuzithatha kanjani izindlela ezintsha zokusebenza, ukwakha phezu kokubambisana okukhona okuphumelele, ukubambisana okuhambisana nokweseka kwabo ezinhlelweni zezwe namasu okungawezwe futhi aholwa yilo. Ukusayidwa kuSDG3 GAP kuyiGavi ubudlelwane bomgomo; Indawo yezezimali zomhlaba yabesifazane izingane nenstha (GFF); uxhasomali lomhlaba lokulwa kengculaza,isifo sofuba nomalaleveva(Global Fund); I-International Labour Organisation (ILO);
translations.reference: Collaborating locally is key for progress globally towards health-related SDGs A new progress report is being jointly released today by the 13 signatory agencies of the Global Action Plan for Healthy Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP). The report entitled “What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?” reflects four years of joint work by major multilateral agencies outlining what has and has not worked in strengthening collaboration and support to accelerate country progress towards health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). This year marks the halfway point toward the SDGs, but the world is lagging behind to reach the global goals. Since its launch in 2019, the SDG3 GAP enabled creation of new collaboration structures between the signatory agencies in key areas such as sustainable financing and primary health care among others. At least 67 countries engaged in on one or more of the SDG3 GAP accelerator themes. "We have made important progress, but we still have a long path to travel to improve the way that multilateral organizations work together to support countries. We must listen to what countries tell us and act upon their guidance,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General and Chair of the SDG3 GAP Principals Group. “I thank the partners for their collaboration and for the honest self-assessments contained in this report. "The report provides six key recommendations to ensure that countries benefit from a more streamlined support and to realize the commitments made in the SDG3 GAP. These include: continue the SDG3 GAP improvement cycle for health by seeking the views of Member States on how we collaborate at country-level and responding to related recommendations; maintain GAP as a platform for collaboration; foster stronger collaboration at the country level on primary health care and explore new thematic topics such as climate resilient health systems; jointly apply new approaches at country level such as the delivery for impact approach; engage more with civil society; and work with Member States to strengthen incentives for collaboration through political leadership, governance direction and funding to support collaboration. Key findings of the report will feed into discussions of the next United Nations General Assembly SDG Summit and the High-level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage in September 2023. The months leading up to these key events provide an opportunity for a joint push by Member States and multilateral agencies to identify ways on how to implement the recommendations and to prepare the ground to fully leverage stronger collaboration and accelerate progress towards the health-related SDGs in the second half of the SDG timeframe. Note to the editor: The SDG3 GAP describes how the 13 signatory agencies will adopt new ways of working, building on existing successful collaborations, and jointly align their support around national plans and strategies that are country-owned and -led. The signatories to the SDG3 GAP are Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance; Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (Global Fund); International Labour Organization (ILO);
translations.hypothesis: Collaboration is crucial for achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly SDG 3, which focuses on health and well-being for all. A new report, released today by the 13 signatory organizations to the Global Action Plan for Health Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP), examines the progress made over the past four years. The report, titled "What worked? What didn't? What's next?", assesses the effectiveness of collaborative efforts and partnerships in advancing SDG 3. The report highlights the importance of collaboration in achieving the goals, but also identifies areas where more progress is needed. "We need to learn from each other and build on our successes," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO and Chair of the SDG3 GAP. "I thank all the partners for their commitment to this important work." The report provides valuable recommendations for strengthening collaboration and ensuring that SDG 3 is on track to be achieved by 2030. These recommendations include: Strengthening collaboration within the SDG3 GAP by sharing best practices and responding to challenges; Maintaining the GAP as a platform for collaboration; Exploring new approaches to collaboration, such as using technology to improve access to health services; Engaging with communities to promote health and well-being; and Working with Member States to strengthen political commitment to collaboration. The findings of the report will be presented at the upcoming United Nations General Assembly SDG Summit and the Universal Health Coverage Forum in 2023. These events provide an opportunity for Member States and other stakeholders to share their experiences and learn from each other. The report also highlights the importance of collaboration in achieving the goals of SDG 3. By working together, we can ensure that everyone has access to the health and well-being they need to thrive.

translations.source: Uhlelo lokuhlanganyela lweUnited Nations lwe HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); I-United Nation Development Fund (UNDP); I-United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA); I-UnitedNations Children Fund(UNICEF); Unitaid; I-United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women); Iqoqo lebhange lomhlaba; Uhlelo lwezokudla emhlabeni (WFP); kanye NeNhlangano Yezempilo Emhlabeni (WHO). Bona umbiko ogcwele, Yikuphi okusebenzile? Yikuphi okulandelayo?
translations.reference: Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); United Nations Development Programme (UNDP); United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA); United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF); Unitaid; United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women); World Bank Group; World Food Programme (WFP); and World Health Organization (WHO). See full report, What Worked? What’s next?

A comprehensive report, What has worked? What is next?

translations.source: Imiphumela yocwaningo olusha ikhombisa izinhlelo zempilo ziqala ukuba ngcono esikhathini sobhubhane Ngemva kweminyaka emithathu yobhubhane lweCOVID-19 izinhlelo zempilo emazweni seziqale ukukhombisa izinkomba ezinkulu zokuvuseleleka kwezinhlelo zempilo, ngokweWHO umbiko wesikhashana emzuliswaneni wesine wocwaningo lephalisi yomhlaba ekuqhubekeni kwemisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile ngesikhathi sobhubhane lweCOVID-19: Lwezi 2022-Masingana 2023. Ngokuqala kuka-2023 amazwe abike ukwenzeka kokwehla kokuphazamiseka ekulethweni kwendlela yemisebenzi yezempilo,kodwa kwavezwa isidingo sokutshala kokungalungiswa nokubekezela kakhulu kwikusasa. Kulamazwe angu-139 aphendule kumzuliswane wesine wokuzwakala kocwaningo lweWHO, amazwe abike ukuqhubeka kokuphazamiseka emisebenzini yekota esilinganisweni. Emazweni angu-84 lapho ukuhlaziya okudumile kungenzeka,iphesenti lemisebenzi ephazamisekile yehlile ngesilinganiso esisuka ku-56% ngoNtulikazi kuya kuMandulo 2020 ngo-23 % ngoLwezi 2022-Masingana 2023. Ukuqhubeka kokuphazamiseka kungenxa yakho kokubili ukufuneka nokusaplaya izinkinga eziseceleni, okufaka amazinga aphansi okunakekelwa kwezempilo emphakathini okudingayo kanye nomkhawulo wokubakhona kwabasebenzi bezempilo nezinsizakusebenza ezinye zokunakekelwa kwezempilo njengokuvulwa kwemitholampilo noma ukubakhona kwesitoko semithi nemikhiqizo. "Izindaba ezamukelwayo ukuthi izinhlelozempilo emzaweni amaningi ziqala ukuvuselela imisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile ezigidini zabantu abawugejile ngesikhathi sobhubhane," kusho uDkt Rudi Eggers, Umqondisi weWHO weMisebenzi yeZempilo yoKuhlanganisiwe. "Kodwa sidinga ukuqinisekisa ukuthi wonke amazwe ayaqhubeka nokuvala izikhala ukuvuselela imisebenzi yezempilo,nokusebenzisa izifundo abazifundile ukwakha izinhlelo zempilo ezizilungiselele nokubekezela kwekusasa". Izimpawu zokuqala eziqaphelekayo ekuvuseleleni Kulolu cwaningo olusha,amazwe ambalwa abike ngokungenhloso isikalo sasemuva sokuthola kuzinkundla zonke umsebenzi wokuletha nokusebenza kwezempilo yomphakathi okubalulekile kusukela 2020-2021 ukubikwa kukhombisa ukubaluleka kwegxathu kubuyelwe emazingeni angaphambi kobhubhane ukulethwa kokusebenza nokusebenza okukhulu kwezinhlelo. Ekupheleni kuka-2022, amazwe amaningi abike izimpawu ezingacacile zokuvuselelwa kokusebenza okufaka ukusebenza kwezocansi, ukuzala, abakhullwe, abazelwe, izingane nezempilo yentsha, ukudla okunempilo ukugomma,izifo ezithelelanayo (okufaka umalaleveva, iHIV, iTB nezinye izifo ezithelelana ngokocansi) izifo ezidumile ezinganakiwe,izifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo,ukuphathwa kwengqondo, okwengqondo nesifo sokusebenzisa izidakamiza, ukunakekelwa kwabantu abadala nokunakekelwa okulekelelayo nokwesiko. Inombolo yamazwe abika ukuphazamiseka ohlelweni lwawo lesaplayi sheni ezweni okungafika kuhhafu (29 ka-59 wamazwe aphendulayo) okunganekota (18 wa-66 wamazwe) aphedulayo kulonyaka odlule. Ngaphandle kwezinkomba zokuvuselela,ukuphazamiseka komsebenzi kuyaqhubeka emazweni onke namazinga emali engenayo kuzona zonke izinhlelo soomsebenzi wokulatha nokulandelela umsebenzi ezindaweni. Amazwe aphinde abhekane nokunyuka komsebenzi okumele wenziwe - ikakhulukazi emisebenzini yokubheka, ukuxilonga nokwelapha izifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo - okungaholela kumiphumela engemihle uma abantu bebambezeleka ukuthola ukunakekelwa ngesikhathi. Ukuvuselelwa kwemisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile kubucayi ngenxa yokuphazamiseka - okufaka imisebenzi yokugqugquzela ezempilo, ukuvinjwa kwezifo, ukwelapha, ukuvuselelwa kwesimilo nephaliyeshini - kungaba nokukhulu umthelela ohlukene wezempil nezinga lomuntu ukunobhubhane qobo ikakhulukazi kubantu babuthaka. Ukuhlanganisa imisebenzi yeCOVID-19 emisebenzini yezempilo ebalulekile elinye igxathu elibalulekile eliya ekuvuselelweni kohlelo noguquko,amazwe amaningi asebe nenqubekela phambili ekuhlanganiseni imisebenzi yeCOVID-19 endleleni yokuletha imisebenzi yezempilo. Cishe u-80%-90% wamazwe ahlanganyele ngokuphelele ekugonyweni kweCOVID-19 ukuxilonga nemisebenzi yokuphathwa kwecala kanye nemisebenzi yesimo sangemuva kweCOVID-19 endleleni yomsebenzi wokuletha. Kuyafana amazwe amaningi (u-80% wamazwe awu-83 aphendulile) abike okungenani okuncane ekukhuphukeni kokuthola amathuluzi abalulekile eCovid-19 (isibonelo ukuxilongwa kweCOVID-19, ukwelaphangxube, imigomo namathuluzi okuzivikela siqu - PPE), ngezinkinga zokusebenza kwezempilo nokwentuleka koxhasomali okumele izivimbo ezijwayeleke kakhulu. Ukuqhubeka kokusekwa kuyadingeka ukuvuselela,ukubekezela nokuzilungiselela Amazwe amaningi aseqalile asebenzisa asebekufundile ngesikhathi sobhubhane lweCOVID-19 okufaka ngezikhungo zenamba yokusbenza kokusha ukuphazamisa amasu endleleni yokulethwa kwezempilo. Lokhu kufaka ukubekwa kwezindlela zokwelapha ngefoni,ukugqugquzela ukunakekelwa usekhaya nokungenelela kokuzinakekela, izindlela zokuqinisa ukubakhona kwabasebenzi bezempilo, amandla nezindlela zokweseka, ukwenza kabusha ekuthengeni nokuletha imithi namasaplayi ezinye izindlela zokuxhumana nomphakathi ukubambisana nabanikezeli bomkhakha ozimele nowomphakathi. Amakota amathathu ezwe abaike ukufakwa koxhasomali olwengeziwe oluya ekuvuselelweni kohlelo lwesikhathi eside, ukubekezela nokuzilungiselela. Amazwe agcizelele isidingo sokwesekwa kweWHO ekubhekaneni nezinkinga ezisele ngokwengqikithi yeCOVID-19 nangale,ikakhulukazi okujwayele ukuhlobana nokuqinisa ukusebenza kwezempilo, ukwakha amandla okubheka imisebenzi yezempilo, ukwakha ukunakekelwa kwezempilo kokuqala okuncike kuzindlela zokunakekela, ukubusa, inqubomgomo nokuhlela nokuhlela ezezimali noxhasomali. Inothi kubahleli: Emzuliswaneni wesine wocwaningo lwephasile yomhlaba weWHO, amazwe angu-222, izindawo bamenywa ukuba baphendule kucwaningo oluncike kuwebhu eqinisikesiwe phakathi kukaLwezi 2022 noMasingana 2023. Ucwaningo lwesine lulandele kucwaningo lwephasile edlule yango-2020 no2021 lweWHO: uMzuliswano 1 (Nhlaba-Mandulo 2020) uMzuliswano 2 (Masingana-Ndasa 2021) noMzuliswano 3 (Lwezi-Zibandlela 2021) okwakhombisa ukukhulu bokuthi ubhubhane lalukhahlameza ukuqhubeka kwemisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile nokuthi amazwe enza kanjani.
translations.reference: New survey results show health systems starting to recover from pandemic After three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries have started showing the first major signs of health system recovery, according to the WHO interim report on the “Fourth round of the global pulse survey on continuity of essential health services during the COVID-19 pandemic: November 2022–January 2023”. By early 2023, countries reported experiencing reduced disruptions in the delivery of routine health services, but highlighted the need to invest in recovery and stronger resilience for the future. Among the 139 countries that responded to the fourth round of WHO’s pulse survey, countries reported continued disruptions in almost one-quarter of services on average. In 84 countries where trend analysis is possible, the percentage of disrupted services declined on average from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022- January 2023. Persisting disruptions are due to both demand- and supply-side factors, including low levels of health care-seeking in communities as well as limited availability of health workers and other health-care resources such as open clinics or available stocks of medicines and products. “It is welcome news that health systems in the majority of countries are starting to restore essential health services for millions of people who missed them during the pandemic,” said Dr Rudi Eggers, WHO Director for Integrated Health Services. “But we need to ensure that all countries continue to close this gap to recover health services, and apply lessons learnt to build more prepared and resilient health systems for the future”. First notable signs of recovery In this new survey, fewer countries reported intentionally scaling back access across all service delivery platforms and essential public health functions since 2020-2021 reporting, showing an important step to return to pre-pandemic levels of service delivery and broader system functioning. By the end of 2022, most countries reported partial signs of service recovery, including in services for sexual, reproductive, maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health; nutrition; immunization; communicable diseases (including malaria, HIV, TB, and other sexually-transmitted infections); neglected tropical diseases; noncommunicable diseases; management of mental, neurological and substance use disorders; care for older people; and traditional and/or complementary care. The number of countries reporting disruption to their national supply chain system reduced from nearly half (29 of 59 responding countries) to about a quarter (18 of 66 responding countries) within the last year. Despite signs of recovery, service disruptions persist across countries in all regions and income levels, and across most service delivery settings and tracer service areas. Countries are also dealing with increasing service backlogs – most frequently in services for screening, diagnosis and treatment of noncommunicable diseases – which can lead to negative consequences as people are delayed access to timely care. Recovering essential health service delivery is critical because disruptions – including to services for health promotion, disease prevention, diagnosis, treatment, rehabilitation and palliation – may have even greater adverse health effects at population and individual level than the pandemic itself, especially among vulnerable populations. Integrating COVID-19 services into essential health services In another important step towards system recovery and transition, most countries have made progress in integrating COVID-19 services into routine health service delivery. About 80-90% of countries have fully integrated COVID-19 vaccination, diagnostic and case management services as well as services for post COVID-19 condition into routine service delivery. Still, most countries (80% of 83 responding countries) reported at least one bottleneck to scaling up access to essential COVID-19 tools (e.g. COVID-19 diagnostics, therapeutics, vaccines and personal protective equipment - PPE), with health workforce issues and lack of funding representing the most common barriers. Further support needed for recovery, resilience and preparedness Most countries have started to apply what they have learnt during the COVID-19 pandemic, including through the institutionalization of a number of innovative service disruption mitigation strategies into routine health service delivery. These include deployment of telemedicine approaches, promotion of home-based care or self-care interventions, approaches for strengthening health workers availability, capacities and support mechanisms, innovations in procuring and delivering medicines and supplies, more routine community communications, and partnerships with private sector providers. Three quarters of countries reported additional funding allocation towards longer term system recovery, resilience and preparedness. Countries expressed need for WHO support to address remaining challenges in the COVID-19 context and beyond, most frequently related to health workforce strengthening, building the monitoring capacities of health services, designing primary health care -- oriented models of care, governance, policy and planning and financial planning and funding. Note to editors: In the fourth round of WHO’s global pulse survey, 222 countries, territories and areas were invited to respond to a standardized web-based survey between November 2022 and January 2023. The fourth survey followed up on WHO’s previous 2020 and 2021 pulse surveys: Round 1 (May-September 2020), Round 2 (January-March 2021), and Round 3 (November-December 2021) which showed the extent to which the pandemic was affecting the continuity of essential health services and how countries are taking action.
translations.hypothesis: New research findings show that health systems are starting to recover from the COVID-19 pandemic. After three years of disruption, health systems in many countries are showing signs of recovery, according to a recent WHO report. The report, which was released on Friday, found that health systems in 139 countries are still facing challenges, but that there is a growing sense of optimism about the future. In the first half of 2023, most countries reported a decrease in the number of people who were unable to access healthcare, according to the WHO report. However, the report also found that there is still a need for increased investment in health infrastructure and workforce. The report is based on data from the WHO's Global Health Security Agenda, which collects data from countries on a variety of health security indicators. The data shows that the pandemic has had a significant impact on health systems, but that there is also a growing awareness of the need to prepare for future health emergencies.

translations.source: Ngesikhathi ucwaningo lwephasile lunemikhawulo njengokubika ukuchema nokumela,amandla alomzamo ukuthi mukhulu futhi uletha ulwazi ngokuphindekile. Itemu "izwe" kumele liqondwe ukuthi lifaka wonke amazwe, imingcele nezindawo. Abahlaziya okudumile kwaqedwa kuncike ezimpendulweni eziphuma emazweni angu-84, abahlukumezi noma izindawo ezaphendula okungenani kusahluko socwaningo olulodwa kuyona yonke imizuliswano emine yocwaningo.
translations.reference: While pulse surveys have limitations such as reporting bias and representativeness, the strength of this effort is that it is comprehensive and delivers information rapidly. The term “country” should be understood to include all countries, territories and areas. Trend analyses was completed based on responses from 84 countries, territories, or areas that responded to at least one survey section in all four pulse survey rounds.
translations.hypothesis: During the initial phase of the study, there were limitations such as the need for detailed documentation and the challenge of collecting data from a large and diverse population. The term "nation" should be understood to encompass all countries, regions, and communities. The most common types of research questions addressed in the 84 surveyed countries included questions related to health, education, and economic development.

translations.source: Isitatimende sokubungaza ukuphela kweminyaka engu-50 yeHRP: iSweden ne-United Kingdom of Great Britain neNorthen Ireland Ukubamba isimo seNqubomgomo neKomidi lokuXhumana (PCC) Amalungu abungaza ukuphela kuka-50 HRP nokuzuza okuningi, iSweden neUnited Kingdom of Great Britain neNorthern Ireland ukufunda ukuqapheleka okulandelayo kokwamukela nokuhalalisa emhlanganweni wesi-36 weHRP PCC. Isizukulwane sethu sesibone inqubekela phambili enkulu kusayensi nezobuchwepheshe ukuthi konke okuqhubekayo kwesizukulwane ndawonye, nokubona okwemfundo okumbalwa,ezobuchwepheshe, ezokwelapha noguquko lwenhlalakahle luqhutshwa ukuguquka kokuvikela kulandelwa ukwenzangcono kwezempilo yokuzala, ukwenza kabusha kwezempilo kanye namalungelo nokulingana ngokobulili. Kepha ukucabanga izingqinamba emkhakheni weSRHR uqhubeke futhi kuphikiswane,okunye ukuqhubeka kusembuzweni futhi namhlanje. Sincoma abasebenzi beHRP ababambisani nabeseki kwabakutholile okuningi kuze kube yinamhlanje nokubona izinto ezinkulu ezenzekile ngesikhathi esinguhhafu wekhulunyaka ekukhiqizeni ubufakazi obeseka ukungena okuphelele nokulinganayo kwezempilo namalungelo ezocansi nokuzala. Singathanda ukuveza imikhakha emikhulu emithathu yokuzuziwe Okokuqala ucwaningo olusezingeni eliphezulu lweHRP lube nomthelela obonakalayo kunqubomgomo yezempilo yomphakathi,amazinga, nokwenza emhlabeni wonke okubhekene nezidingo zokuguquka kusibalo sabantu esahlukene. Okwesibili HRP iqapheleke ngeHRP Alliance, isiqinise izikhungo zocwaningpo endaweni nasemhlabeni,kwavumela abacwaningi abaningi ukuthi bathole amakhono okucwabinga ukuqinisekisa ubunhalo obuphezulu bocwaningo lweSRHR ukuthi liqhubeke futhi lunyuke esikhathini esizayo. Okwesithathu,umhlaba uhlelwa yizinkinga, ukuxabana nokunyusa ukuqaba ekulinganeni kobulili, iHRP isize ukubamba isisekelo samalungelo abasefazane nezingane nabantu ababuthaka. I-HRP ihlala iyithuluzi elibalulekile kuhlelo lweUnited Nations yocwaningo lokhukhiqiza kubantu nokuqhubeka nokuba umholi ovelele kwezempilo yezocansi nokuzala emhlabeni. HRP nababambisani bayo sebesisondeze kusithembiso sango-1994 KuNkomfa Yamazwe angaphandle Esibalweni Sabantu neNtuthuko, futhi isimemezelo sibeka amalungelo abantu ikakhulukazi abesifazane namantombazane phakathi nokugcineka kwentuthuko. Sifisa ukuzwakalisa ukubonga nokujabula kwethu kunobhala weHRP, ababambisani, kanye nabaxhasi kanye nohlelokuxhumana lomhlaba ezizindeni zokubambisana nongoti abasiza ukwenza isiqiniseko ukuthi imizamo inikela ekwenzeni umehluko kubantu besifazane, amadoda, izingane, intsha, nemiphakathini eyahlukene iHRP izimisele ngokusiza. Ngokubambela amalunga ePCC sivumeleni siqinisekise ukubambisana kwethu nani ukweseka okuzuziwe kweHRP ekuphumeleleni okuncike ku-ajenda 2030.
translations.reference: Statement of celebration for HRP’s 50th anniversary: Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland To capture the spirit of Policy and Coordination Committee (PCC) members celebrating HRP’s 50th anniversary and its numerous achievements, Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland read the following note of appreciation and congratulations at the 36th meeting of the HRP PCC. Our generation has seen more progress in science and technology than all preceding generations together and witnessed several intellectual, technological, medical and social transformation spearheaded by the contraceptive revolution and followed by advances in reproductive health, health innovations as well as rights and gender equality. However, considerable challenges in the field of SRHR continue, and, arguably, some advances are in question again today. We applaud HRP-staff, partners and supporters on the numerous achievements to date and recognise the enormous strides accomplished during half a century in producing the evidence that supports full and equal access to sexual and reproductive health and rights. We would like to outline three major categories of achievements: Firstly, the high-quality research by HRP has had tangible impact on public health policy, standards, and practice worldwide addressing the evolving needs of diverse populations. Secondly, HRP, notably through the HRP Alliance, has strengthened research institutions locally and globally, and, enabled numerous researchers to acquire the research skills that ensure that high-quality SRHR research can continue, and indeed increase, into the future. Thirdly, in a world beset by crisis, conflict and increasing resistance to gender equality, HRP has facilitated the upholding of the basic rights of women and girls and vulnerable populations. HRP remains the main instrument within the United Nations system for research in human reproduction and continues to be an exceptional leader in global sexual and reproductive health. HRP and its partners have brought us closer to the promise of the 1994 International Conference on Population and Development and its call to put the rights of individuals, especially women and girls, at the heart of sustainable development. We want to express our appreciation and gratitude to the HRP secretariat, its partners, co-sponsors and the global network of collaborating centres and experts helping to ensure its efforts contribute to making a difference to the women, men, children, adolescents and various communities HRP is committed to serve. On behalf of PCC members, allow us to reaffirm our partnership with you to support the achievements of HRP, upon which the success of the 2030 Agenda depends.
translations.hypothesis: Statement on the 50th Anniversary of HRP: Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland Commemorating the achievements of the 50th HRP and beyond, Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland will participate in the 36th HRP PCC meeting. Our generation is witnessing a significant shift in science and technology, with advancements in education, technology, healthcare, and social development leading to improved health outcomes, increased life expectancy, and greater equality. However, challenges remain in the area of SRHR, and progress is not always linear. We commend the HRP for its continued efforts to address these challenges and for its commitment to promoting the health and well-being of all. We would like to highlight three key areas where the HRP has made a significant impact. First, the HRP has played a crucial role in the development of evidence-based policies and programs for SRHR. Second, the HRP has supported the development of research and training programs in SRHR, which have helped to build capacity and expertise in this field. Third, the HRP has been instrumental in advocating for the rights of women and girls, and for the elimination of violence against them. The HRP has been a vital partner in the United Nations' efforts to promote SRHR, and we are grateful for its continued commitment to this important cause. We would like to express our gratitude to the HRP, its partners, and its stakeholders for their hard work and dedication.

translations.source: I-WHO yethula umzamo omusha wokwenza ngcono ukuzilungiselela kobhubhane Ukusiza amazwe ukuthi azilungisele okungcono kwekusasa lobhubhane,WHO yethule umzamo namahlanje ozonikeza imiyalelo yokuhlela ngokuhlanganisa ngokuphendula kunoma iyiphi iphathojini yokuphefumula njengomkhuhlane noma ikhorona. I-Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative, noma iPRET, ihlanganisa amathuluzi akamuva nezindlela zokufunda okwabelwana ngazo kanye nesenzo esiyiqoqo esisungulwe phakathi nobhadane lweCOVID-19 nezinye izimo eziphuthumayo zakamuva zezempilo zomphakathi. Ngomzamo iWHO izosebenzisa indlela yokudlulisela ukuyala amazwe ekuhleleni ubhubhane,ngoba kukhona amandla amaningi namandla amaningi afanayo kumaqoqo ephathojini. I-PRET iphendula isimemezelo somuyalelo wezobuchwepheshe nokwesekwa kokugqugquzelwa nokuqiniswa kokuhlanganisa ukuzilungiselela nokuphendula,njengova kuveziwe kuzinqumo zeWorld Health Assembly. Ububhane lweCOVID-19 nezinye izimo eziphuthumayo zezempilo zikhombisile ukuthi amazwe adinga ukuthi alungele ukubhekana nezinsongo zezifo ezithathelwanayo, ngezinhlelo zokuzilungiselela ezilungiselelwe ngesandla kanye nokuxhumana okungcono nokusebenzisana neminye imikhakha efana nezolimo. "Ukuzilungiselela, ukuvikela nemisebenzi yokuphendula akumele kube yisifundazwe somkhakha wezempilo kuphela," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO. "Njengeziphuthumayo zempilo zinomthelela kumikhakha eminingi, ngakho nokuzilungiselela kwethu nemizamo yokuphendula mayilingane nemikhakah, izinhlobo namagciwane. Kubucayi futhi ukuthi ukukhuluma komphakathi nokulingana kuphakathi kwemizamo yethu, ikakhulukazi kulabo bantu abagcindezelekile futhi abasengcupheni enkulu." I-PRET iwuguquko endleleni yeWHO ekuzilungiseleni kobhubhane ngokusebenzisa indlela yelensi yokudlulisela, ukunokugxila ezifweni ezithile. I-WHO izoqhubeka nokuthuthukisa nokusabalalisa imiyalelo ezifweni ezithile njengoba kudingeka. Ukwethulwa komzamo kuzokwenzeka ngosuku lokugcina lwemihlangano abangaphezu kuka-120 abamele Member State, ababambisani, noNobhala weWHO, ukuchaza indlela eya phambili yePRET okufaka ukuthuthuka kwebalazwe lokwenza lomhlaba kokuzilungiselela lobhubhane lwegciwane lokuphefumula. Imizamo yePRET isifundo sokuqala sigxile kumagciwane pokuphefumula, okufaka umkhuhlane, igciwane lekhorona namagciwane ezokuphefumula. Ngokunikezwa ubhubhane oluqhubekayo nokwesabisa okungenzeka emkhuhlaneni, lemojuli izokwenza amazwe abheke ngokucophelela, ukuhlola, ukubuyekeza imizamo yokuhlela ubhubhane lwezokuphefumula ukuqinisekisa banamandla okusebenzayo, namandla akhona. Uhlelo lusendleleni lokuhlonza iqoqo elilandelayo lamagciwane, njengama-arbovirus, ukuthi kubhekwane nawo ngaphansi kwalomzamo. Lokhu kuzolandela okuhamba phambili okuhlonziwe ngeziphakamiso eziyishumi zokuqinisa ukwakhiwa komhlaba kokuzilungiselela kokuphuthuma kwezempilo, ukuphendula nokubekezela (HEPR). Umzamo wePRET ukuyisa esikhathini esisha sokuzilungiselela kobhubhane nokumela uguquko lwemisebenzi ngqo yeWHO ukweseka wonke amaMember State ekuqiniseni ezempilo zokuzilungiselela okuphuthumayo, ukuvikela namandla okuphendula nokukwazi. Umzamo ungaphinde usize ukusebenzisa izinjongo nokuhlinzekwa kwePandemic Accord okwamanje okusaxoxwa ngayo yiWHO Member State. Kulandela lokho okufakiwe nezingxoxo emihlanganweni yababambe iqhaza, iWHO imema ababambisani kulesimemezelo somhlaba sokwenza ukuwandisa ukuzilungiselela kobhubhane legciwane lokuphefumula. Kufuneka ukwenza AmaMember State eWHO, nababambisani basebenza ndawonye ukuhlonza nokuhambisa phambili ukwenza izenzo zokuqinisa izwe, isifunda, nokuzilungiselela komhlaba kwezobhubhane nokuvela kwezifo ezithelelanayo ezisabisayo. Siyayibona indima yomphakathi nayo yonke imikhakha sidinga ukulinganisa nokugcina umfutho ekupheleni kwesakile yokusaba nokunganaki lokho kujwayelekile ekuzilungiseleni kobhubhane nokuphendula. Sikhumbula izifundo zobhubhane olwedlule ne-ephidemikhi, ikakhulukazi lezo ezidalwa amagciwane okuphefumula. Lawo magciwane azoqhubeka nokuletha ukusabisa komhlaba okukhulu ngamandla okudala izinkinga ezinkulu nokufa, izinhlelo zempilo ezingaphezu kwamandla, ukwehlisa ezomnotho zomhlaba, ukwengeza ukungalingani, okukhona ekungeneni kumathuluzi omabili okuvikela ubhubhane nokunakekelwa ezempilo kubona bonke abantu. Lesi yisimemezelo sokwenza ukuqhubeza ukuzilungiselela kobhubhane nokuvela kokwesaba emhlabeni.
translations.reference: WHO launches new initiative to improve pandemic preparedness To help countries better prepare for future pandemics, WHO launched a new initiative today that provides guidance on integrated planning for responding to any respiratory pathogen such as influenza or coronaviruses. The new Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative, or PRET, incorporates the latest tools and approaches for shared learning and collective action established during the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent public health emergencies. Through the initiative, WHO will use a mode of transmission approach to guide countries in pandemic planning, given that many capacities and capabilities are common among groups of pathogens. PRET answers the call for technical guidance and support for promoting and strengthening integrated preparedness and response, as outlined in World Health Assembly resolutions. The COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies have shown that countries need to be operationally ready to respond to infectious disease threats, with tailored preparedness plans in hand and better coordination and collaboration with other sectors such as agriculture. “Preparedness, prevention, and response activities must not be the province of the health sector alone,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Just as health emergencies have impacts across many sectors, so must our preparedness and response efforts span sectors, disciplines and pathogens. It is critical, too, that community engagement and equity are the centre of our efforts, especially for those populations that are marginalized and most at risk.” PRET is an evolution in WHO’s approach to pandemic preparedness through the application of a mode of transmission lens, rather than a focus on specific diseases. WHO will continue to develop and disseminate guidance on specific diseases as needed. The launch of the initiative is taking place on the last day of meetings with over 120 individuals representing Member States, partners, and the WHO secretariat, to define the path forward for PRET, including the development of a global implementation roadmap for respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. The PRET Initiative’s first module focuses on respiratory pathogens, including influenza, coronaviruses, and respiratory syncytial virus. Given the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and the possible threat of avian influenza, this module will enable countries to critically review, test, and update their respiratory pandemic planning efforts to ensure they have the functional capacities and capabilities in place. A process is underway to identify the next group of pathogens, such as arboviruses, to be addressed under this initiative. This will follow priorities identified through the ten proposals to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience (HEPR). The PRET Initiative ushers in a new era for pandemic preparedness and represents an evolution of WHO’s core activities to support all Member States in strengthening health emergency preparedness, prevention, and response capacities and capabilities. The initiative can also serve to operationalize the objectives and provisions of the Pandemic Accord, which is currently being negotiated by WHO Member States. Following inputs and discussions among meeting participants, WHO invites stakeholders to this global call to action to advance respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. Call to Action WHO, Member States, and partners are working together to identify and implement priority actions to strengthen national, regional, and global preparedness for pandemics and emerging infectious disease threats. We recognize the role of communities and all sectors, and the need for harmonized and sustained momentum to end the cycle of panic and neglect that is all too common in pandemic preparedness and response. We recall lessons from previous epidemics and pandemics, especially those caused by respiratory pathogens. These pathogens have and will continue to pose a significant global threat with the potential to cause tremendous morbidity and mortality, overwhelm health systems, de-stabilize the global economy, and exacerbate inequities, which exist in the access to both the tools to prevent pandemics and health care for all people. This is a Call to Action to accelerate preparedness for pandemics and emerging threats globally.
translations.hypothesis: The WHO has launched a new initiative to improve pandemic preparedness. The initiative, called the Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative (PRET), aims to provide countries with the tools and resources they need to better prepare for future pandemics. The PRET initiative will use a "one health" approach, which recognizes that human, animal, and environmental health are interconnected. This approach will help countries to identify and address the underlying factors that contribute to the emergence and spread of infectious diseases. The WHO will also use the PRET initiative to share best practices and lessons learned from past pandemics, such as the COVID-19 pandemic. "Pandemics are a global threat, and we need to work together to prepare for them," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the WHO. "The PRET initiative is a step in the right direction, but we need to do more to ensure that all countries have the resources they need to prepare for future pandemics." The PRET initiative is a collaborative effort between the WHO, Member States, and other partners. It will focus on a number of key areas, including surveillance, prevention, and response. The initiative will also work to strengthen the global health security architecture, which is the framework for international cooperation on pandemic preparedness and response. The PRET initiative is a significant step forward in the WHO's efforts to improve pandemic preparedness. It will help to ensure that countries are better prepared to respond to future pandemics, and it will help to save lives.

translations.source: Ukusebenza kokuzilungiselela kuncike kubukhulu bokuhlela nokwenza kokuxhumana. Ukuzimisela kweqoqo kuzenze ezilandelayo kuzobonisa ukuqhubeka okutholakele ngoZibandlela 2025 futhi kuzochazwa (Ukuzilungiselela nokubekezela kokusabisayo okuvelayo (PRET) ukubheka umhlahlandlela: Ukulungiselelwa kokubuyekeziwe ukuhlela okuqinisekisa izenzo zokuhamba phambili nokucabanga izifundo zezigemegeme ezedlule. Ukubona ingcuphe efakwe amagciwane ezokuphefumula, ukuhlela kobhubhane lamagciwane ezokuphefumula ancike kuzingqikithi ezihlonzwe kusifundo sePRET #1: Ukuhlelela ubhubhane lwamagciwane ezokuphefumula kuhamba phambili Ukunyusa ukuxhumana kubabambisani ekuzilungiseleni kobhubhane ukuhlela ngezinhlelo zokuxhumana nokusebenzisana. Lokhu kufaka ukwakha izinhlelo ezilinganayo ukwenza imisebenzi ngokuhlanganyela; Nokwabelana ngolwazi ekwenzeni okuhle, izingqinamba kanye namathuba. Ukunikela ngotshalomali olugcinekile, izimali nokubheka kokulungela ubhubhane ngokugxila ekubhekeni izikhala ezihlonze ngesikhathi sobhubhane olwedlule. Ubhubhane lweCOVID-19 luphendule ukukhombisa ukuthi yikuphi okungazuzwa ngokuzibophezela kwezepolitiki, ukukhuluma komphakathi kanye noxhasomali. Phakathi nalomsebenzi ukuqinisekisa ukulingana ukulungela obhubhane olulandelayo ndawonye. Ukwenza komphakathi wonke kuyadingeka ukwenza inqubekela phambili evezwe kule simemezelo sokwenza. Ukwenza kumele kuqinise imiphakathi ebekezelayo; egcinayo nokugcineka nokwakha izindlela zezinhlelo; nokubambisa kwamandla amakhulu kokuzilungiselela okuphuthumayo nokuphendula. Lesi simemezelo sokwenza sakhishwa eKuzilungiseleni nokubekezela kokuvela kokusabisayo (PRET): Umhlangano womhlaba wekusasa lobhubhane lwamagciwane ezokuphefumula obanjwe ngo-24-26 Mbasa 2023 eGeneva, eSwitzerland.
translations.reference: Effective preparedness relies on robust planning and coordinated action. A collective commitment on the following actions will see progress achieved by December 2025 as will be described in the (Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET) monitoring framework: Update preparedness plans that affirm priority actions and that have considered learnings from past events. Recognizing the risk posed by respiratory pathogens, planning for a respiratory pathogen pandemic based on the themes identified in the PRET Module #1: Planning for Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics is a priority Increase connectivity among stakeholders in pandemic preparedness planning through systematic coordination and cooperation. This includes building equitable systems; conducting joint exercises; and sharing information on good practices, challenges, and opportunities. Dedicate sustained investments, financing and monitoring of pandemic preparedness with a particular focus on addressing the gaps identified during past pandemics and epidemics. The COVID-19 pandemic response has demonstrated what can be achieved with political commitment, community engagement and funding. At the heart of this work is to ensure equity to be ready for the next pandemic together. Whole-of-society action is needed to make the progress outlined in this Call to Action. Implementation should therefore strengthen the resilience of communities; maintain, sustain, and build on routine systems; and leverage broader capacities for emergency preparedness and response. This Call to Action was released at the Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET): Global Meeting for Future Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics held on 24-26 April 2023 in Geneva, Switzerland.
translations.hypothesis: Preparation efforts depend on the scale of planning and communication. The effectiveness of the preparation will be demonstrated in the following years and will be reflected in (Future Pandemic Preparedness and Response Plan (FPPR) review: A revised plan to ensure ongoing progress and address lessons learned from previous pandemics. This includes identifying key stakeholders, developing communication strategies, and establishing mechanisms for collaboration. This also involves creating standardized protocols for responding to emergencies, sharing information, and coordinating resources. It also includes securing funding, allocating resources, and focusing on preparedness efforts based on historical data. The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the importance of self-reliance, community engagement, and international cooperation. In addition to these efforts, it is important to ensure that all stakeholders are prepared for the next pandemic. This requires ongoing monitoring and evaluation of preparedness efforts. It also requires the development of sustainable and resilient systems, as well as the ability to respond quickly and effectively to emerging threats. This plan for pandemic preparedness and response was adopted at the World Health Organization's Global Pandemic Preparedness and Response Summit, which was held in Geneva, Switzerland, on 24-26 May 2023.

translations.source: I-WHO inxenxa ukunyuswa kokwenza ngamathuluzi aphakanyisiwe ukulwa nomalaleveva Ukumaka uSuku lukaMalaleveva eMhlabeni, Inhlangano Yezempilo Yomhlaba (WHO) izokhipha isimememzelo sokunyusa ukwenza kokungenelela okukhona nokusha ukusindisa izimpilo kumalaleveva. Okungafika kuzigidi ezingu-1.5 izingane ezisengcupheni enkulu yokugula nokufa okusuka kumalaleveva eGhana, eKenya naseMalawi manje sebethole umgomo wokuqala kamalaleveva, RTS, S/AS01 (RTS,S), siyabonga kuhlelo lokuhlola oluqhubekayo oluxhunyaniswa yiWHO. Ukuhlolwa komgomo wamalaleveva, owethulwe ngo-2019,kwenyusa ukulingana kokuthola ukuvikelwa kumalaleveva kulabo ababuthaka kakhulu futhi kusindisa izimpilo. Ukwenziwe kakhulu, iWHO ilinganisela ukuthi umgomo kamalaleveva ungasingisa izimpilo eziyizinkulungwane eziyishumi zezingane ngonyaka. "Sinamathuluzi ukwehlisa umalaleveva,iphekheji yokungenenela ebandakanya ukulawula ivektha,imithi yokuvikela, ukuhlola nokwelapha," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO. "Lokhu kulandelwa ngumgomo ophephile nosebenzayo kamalaleveva, ongasindisa izimpilo eziyizinkulungwane eziyishumi zezingane unyaka ngamunye. Ngotshalomali olugcinekile nokunyuka kwemizamo ukufika kulabo abasengcupheni kakhulu, ukuqedwa kwamalaleveva emazweni amaningi sekuyafika. "Usuku lukaMalaleveva eMhlabeni 2023 lumakwe ngaphansi kwengqikithi, "Isikhathi sokuwqeda umalaleveva, tshala, yenza kabusha yenza". Kule ngqikithi, iWHO inxenxa okunye ukwenza okusebenzayo kwamathuluzi akhona namasu okuvikela, ukuxilonga nokwelapha umalaleveva, ikakhulukazi kulesosibalo esingcindezelekile. Ngokombiko wokugcina wamalaleveva emhlabeni,oshicilelwe ngoZibandlela 2022, bekunesilinganiso sika-247 wezigidi zamacala amasha kamalaleveva ngo-2021. I-WHO African Region iyaqhubeka ukuthwala umthwalo onzima wesifo- okuballelwa esilinganiswenisa ku-95% wamacala kamalaleveva (u-234 wezigidi) no-96% wokufa (593 000) ngo-2021. Kungafika ku-80% ukufa okudalwa umalaleveva eSifundeni se-Afrika obukukubantwana abangaphansi kweminyaka emihlanu. Amasu amasha namathuluzi Amazweasenze inqubekela phambili ekwandiseni ukungena kumisebenzi yamalaleveva kusibalo sabantu esisengcupheni kakhulu. Ngaphandle kokuqhubeka abantu abaningi abasengcupheni yamalaleveva basantula ukungena emisebenzini engavimba, noma itthole bese yelapha isifo. Izingqinamba ekwandeni kokungena kumisebenzi kamalaleveva sezihlanganisisiwe,ikakhulukazi esu-Saharan Afrika, ngobhubhane lweCOVID-19, ukujika izinkinga zobuntu, uxhasomali olunezivimbo, izinhlelo zokubheka ezintekenteke, ukwehla ekwenzeni kwamathuluzi okulwa ngqo nomalaleveva. Ukubhekana nalokhu okusabisayo nokweseka amazwe ekwakheni izinhlelo zokubekezela zamalaleveva, iWHO isanda kushicilela amasu amasha nomhlahlandlela: isu elisha lelinesidakamizwa esingenamalaleveva ukuvika eAfrika; umzamo omusha wokumisa ukusabalala kweAnophelesi stephensi ezindaweni zasemadolobheni; umhlahlandlela omusha, uthuthukiswe ndawonye yiWHO neUN-Habitat, ukuyalela babaholi bedolobha ekulawulweni kwemalalaeveva edolobheni. ithuluzi elisha ukusiza amazwe ahlola umalaleveva wabio wezinhlelo zokubheka nokuhlonza izindawo zotshalomali I-WHO iphinde yanyusa ukwethembeka, ukubalula nokuthola iziphakamiso zemalaleveva. Ukuhlanganiswa kwemiyalelo yeWHO kwamalaleveva sekukhona manje ngezinkundla ezimbili zedijithali: iMAGICapp kanye ne-"MalariaToolkit" isisetshenziswa. I-WHO igqugquzela amazwe ukuthi ahlanganise kuziphakamiso ekuhlelweni kwezifo zendawo kumthelela omkhulu.
translations.reference: WHO urges increased implementation of recommended tools to combat malaria Marking World Malaria Day, the World Health Organization (WHO) is issuing a call for increased implementation of new and existing interventions to save lives from malaria. Nearly 1.5 million children at high risk of illness and death from malaria in Ghana, Kenya and Malawi have now received their first dose of the first malaria vaccine, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), thanks to an ongoing pilot programme coordinated by WHO. The malaria vaccine pilots, launched in 2019, are increasing equity in access to malaria prevention for the most vulnerable and are saving lives. If implemented broadly, WHO estimates that malaria vaccines could save the lives of tens of thousands of children each year. “We have the tools to drive down malaria, a package of interventions that includes vector control, preventive medicines, testing, and treatment,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO. “These are joined by a safe and effective malaria vaccine, which could save the lives of tens of thousands children every year. With sustained investment and scaled-up efforts to reach those most at risk, malaria elimination in many countries is in reach. "World Malaria Day 2023 is being marked under the theme “Time to deliver zero malaria: invest, innovate, implement”. Within this theme, WHO is urging more effective implementation of available tools and strategies to prevent, diagnose and treat malaria, particularly among marginalized populations. According to the latest World malaria report, published in December 2022, there were an estimated 247 million new cases of malaria in 2021. The WHO African Region continues to shoulder the heaviest burden of the disease – accounting for an estimated 95% of all malaria cases (234 million) and 96% of all deaths (593 000) in 2021. Nearly 80% of malaria deaths in the African Region were among children under the age of five. New strategies and tools Countries have made some progress in expanding access to malaria services for most-at-risk populations. Despite some progress, many people at high risk of malaria still lack access to services that can prevent, detect and treat the disease. Challenges in expanding access to malaria services have been compounded, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa, by the COVID-19 pandemic, converging humanitarian crises, restricted funding, weak surveillance systems, and declines in the effectiveness of core malaria-fighting tools. To address these threats and support countries in building more resilient malaria programmes, WHO recently published new strategies and frameworks, including: a new strategy to contain antimalarial drug resistance in Africa; a new initiative to stop the spread of Anopheles stephensi in urban environments; a new framework, developed jointly by WHO and UN-Habitat, to guide city leaders in urban malaria control. a new toolkit to help countries assess their malaria surveillance systems and identify areas for investment WHO has also increased the transparency, flexibility and access to its malaria recommendations. The consolidated WHO Guidelines for malaria are now available through two digital platforms: MAGICapp and the “Malaria Toolkit” app. WHO encourages countries to tailor the recommendations to local disease settings for maximum impact.
translations.hypothesis: The WHO is investigating the use of new tools and strategies to combat malaria. World Health Organization (WHO) will launch a campaign to raise awareness and promote the use of new malaria prevention and treatment tools. Currently, an estimated 1.5 million children in sub-Saharan Africa are at risk of contracting malaria, but there is now a new malaria vaccine, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), which has been shown to be effective in protecting children from the disease. The malaria vaccine, which was developed by the University of Oxford, has been shown to be highly effective in preventing malaria in children, and it is now being rolled out in several African countries. "We have the tools to defeat malaria," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the WHO. "We need to use them effectively to save the lives of millions of children." The WHO estimates that the malaria vaccine could prevent tens of thousands of child deaths each year. "We are making progress in the fight against malaria," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "But we still have a long way to go." The 2023 World Malaria Day is being observed under the theme, "Innovate to eliminate."

translations.source: Izindlela zokungenelela okusha Ukuqhubeka kotshalomali ekuthuthukisweni nokubekwa kwemigomo emisha kamalaleveva namathuluzi esizukulwane esilandelayo kuzobalulekaukuthola okuqondwe ngomalaleveva emhlabeni 2030. Umgomo wesibili kamalaleveva, umgomo iR21/Matrix-M (R21), uma ugunyaziwe ungasiza ukuvala isikhala esikalekayo phakathi kwesaplayi mokufuneka nokuqhubeka ukwehlisa ukugula kwezingane nokufa okudalwa umalaleveva. Kuhamba phambili kuWHO ukuqhubeka ngokubheka kangoti kweR21 malaleveva ngesikhathi sekwengezwe okuphepha okubalulekile,nemininingo kusuka ekuqhubekeni kukaR21 isigaba 3 sesikhona nokinikezelwa kuWHO. Isigaba sokukhiqiza iRTS,S umgomo wamalaleveva emazweni engeziwe eAfrika kulindeleke ukuthi aqale ngokuqala kuka-2024. Endaweni yokulawula ivekitha,kukhona imikhiqizoemisha engu-28 kokuzayo kweR&D. Amathuluzi asabhekisiswa afka, isibonelo, izinhlobo ezintsha zamanethi okulapha izinambuzane, izicuphomziqonde ukudonsa omiyane, isipeshali ripheleli, okwasendlini, (i-eaves tubes) nobunjiniyela bofuzo lwemiyane. Abacwaningi bahambisa phambili ukuthuthuka kokulashwa okungeyona i-ACTS emikhakheni wemithi omele umalaleveva ngenxa yokuphuthuma nokusabalala kokungavuminokuncane kwe-artemisinin. Ukdevelophwa kwemithi yesizukulwane esilandelayo, njenge ACTS, okusebenzisa ukuhlanganiswa kwe-artemisinin nezidakamizwa zimbili, kusendleleni kuzokwehlisa ingcuphe yokungavumi kwesidakamizwa. Amanothi aya kumhleli: ngolwazi olwengeziwe ekukhankaseni ngoSuku lukamalaleveva eMhlabeni lweWHO, vakashela u-http://www.who.int/campains/world-malaria-day/2023
translations.reference: Prospects for new interventions Continued investment in the development and deployment of new malaria vaccines and next-generation tools will be key to achieving the 2030 global malaria targets. A second malaria vaccine, the R21/Matrix-M (R21) vaccine, if approved, could help close the sizable gap between supply and demand and further reduce child illness and death from malaria. It is a priority for WHO to continue the thorough and efficient expert review of the R21 malaria vaccine once additional key safety and efficacy data from the ongoing R21 phase 3 trial are available and provided to WHO. Phased introductions of the RTS,S malaria vaccine in additional countries in Africa are expected to begin in early 2024. In the vector control space, there are 28 new products in the R&D pipeline. Tools under evaluation include, for example, new types of insecticide-treated nets, targeted baits that attract mosquitoes, spatial repellents, lethal house lures (eaves tubes) and genetic engineering of mosquitoes. Researchers are prioritizing the development of non-ACT treatments in the field of antimalarial medicines due to the emergence and spread of partial artemisinin resistance. The development of next-generation medicines, such as "triple ACTs," which use a combination of artemisinin and two partner drugs, is underway to reduce the risk of drug resistance. Notes to the editor:For more information on the WHO World Malaria Day campaign, visit: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023
translations.hypothesis: Strategies for new malaria control: Continued investment in new insecticides, tools, and interventions for the next decade will be crucial to achieving malaria elimination in the 2030 goal. The second insecticide strategy, the R21/Matrix-M (R21) insecticide, if implemented effectively, can help to fill the gap between indoor residual spraying (IRS) and the continued use of insecticide-treated nets (ITNs) to reduce malaria transmission. The WHO is continuing to evaluate the effectiveness of R21 insecticide, as well as the safety and cost-effectiveness of its use in IRS. The RTS,S vaccine, a malaria vaccine developed in Africa, is expected to be rolled out in several African countries starting in 2024. In the area of research and development, there are 28 new insecticide products in development. These include new types of pyrethroids, novel insecticides for controlling insecticide-resistant mosquitoes, and new formulations of existing insecticides. Researchers are also working to develop new tools for malaria control, such as new diagnostic tests and new drugs. The development of new drugs, such as ACTS, which combine artemisinin with two other drugs, is expected to improve the effectiveness of malaria treatment. Notes: For more information on World Malaria Day, please visit http://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023

translations.source: Uhlelo loKudlulisela ezobuchwepheshe i-mRNA iya esigabeni esilandelayo ekuthuthukeni kwayo Ukwethulwa kwehabhu yezobuchwepheshe imRNA indawo e-Afrigen, eNingizimu Afrika. Ababambisani bahlangane eKapa ukuxoxa ngesigaba esilandelayo semRNA umgomo ngokuthuthukisa nokwabelana ngolwazi. Ngaphezu kwababambiqhaza abangu-200 bamazwe angaphandle abasebenza ngoHlelo loKudlulisela lezoBuchwepheshe beMRNA abahlangane eKapa kulelisonto ukuze bahlangane bebonana. Ukujoyinwa iNhlangano yeZempilo eMhlabeni (WHO) uMqondisi Jikelele uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus noDkt Joe Phaahla uNgqongqoshe weZempilo noNgqongqoshe Wezimboni nokuhweba uMnumzane Ebrahim waseNingizimu Afrika ndawonye nezikhulu ezisezingeni eliphezulu eziphuma emazweni axhasile, lomhlangano owehlukile ubheke ukuqhubeka kusukela ukwethula kweWHO neMedicines Patent Pool (MPP) ngoNhlangulana 2021. "Ngiyajabula ukuba lapha eKapa kanye nababambisani bethu ukuzokweseka imodeli egcinekayo yokudluliselwa kobuchwepheshe mRNA ukunikeza amazwe anemali engenayo encane kuya kwephakathi nendawo ukuthola ngokulingana umgomo kanye neminye imikhiqizo yezempilo esindisa izimpilo zabantu," kusho uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO, uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Ngijabule kakhulu ngokuzuziwe kubona bonke ababambe iqhaza kulephrojekthi,ngaphansi kweminyaka emibili sesikhombisile ukuthi uma sisebenza ngokubambisana, siphumelela sonke." Ababambiqhaza bomhlangano kufaka ababambisani bomkhiqizo wasemazweni angu-15 oHlelweni, ongoti abaphambili, imboni abameli bomphakathi kanye nabaxhasimali. Ngesikhathi somhlangano wezinsuku esinhlanu, ababambiqhaza bazokwabelana ngokuqhubeka baxoxe ngokubucayi kwezinto zokugcineka koHlelo njengezinkinga zegunyabunikazi nezindlela zokulawula kanyenesayensi yezobuchwepheshe be-mRNA nokusebenza okubalulekile okuhlobene neLMIC kwezinye izndawo zezifo njengeHIV nesifo sofuba. Ubhubhane lweCOVID-19 sekukale ngaphansi ukuthi ukungalingani okuphelele kukhona ekutholeni imikhiqizo yezempilo, ikakhulukazi imigomo. Kusukela ngoNdasa 2023, ngaphezu kweminyaka emithathu ngemva kokuthi Iwho imemezele iCOVID-19 njengePublic Health of International Concern (PHEIC), u-69.7% wesibalo sabantu emhlabeni sebethole okungenani idosi eyodwa yomgomo weCOVID-19. Okuqaphelekayo lengxenye izohlala ingaphansi kuka-30% emazweni anemali engenayo encane (amaLIC). Loli Hlelo luhlose ukuxhasa ekutholeno okulinganayo emigomweni ye-mRNA ngokunyusa ukuhambisa kwamandla okukhiqiza okugcinekayo kuwona wonke amaLMIC, ukwandisa okwesifunda nokubambisana kwaphakathi nezifunda nokudevelopha nokusiza ukusebenza kwasendaweni ngokwenziwa kokuqeqeshwa nokwesekwa ngongoti. Yikuphi okwehlukile kumodeli yoKudluliselwa kweZobuchwepheshe kwe-mRNA kuhlelo olunezinto eziningi oluvumela ukwabelana ngobuchwepheshe,ukuthola okuningi ukuze ukukhiqizwa ngokwesifunda nendawo kulabo abadingayo kungafinyelela ngokuphindiwe. UDkt Phaahla, uNgqongqoshe weZempilo eNingizimu Afrika, uthe: "Esikubona namhlanje lapha,umzumzu womlando, uHlelo oluhlose ukusiza iLMIC ngohlelokuxhumana lokubambisana emhlabeni. Ngiyajabula ukubona inqubekela phambili eyenziwe esikhathini esifishane kangaka, siyakwamukela ukwesekwa okusuka emazweni amaningi ahlukene-amazwe afana neNingizimu Afrika anokukhiqizwa okuqinile anamandla, futhi sizimisele ukusebenza ndawonye, ukufunda nokwabelana kuthina sonke." Ungqongqoshe Blade Nzimande weMfundo ePhakeme neSayensi, uthe: "Umbono wethu weHabhu yoKudlulisela ubuchwepheshe be-mRNA kungaphezu kweCOVID-19 nje. Amandla okuthi siyakha afuna usizo kuthina ukubhekana nobhubhane lwesikhathi esizayo imigomo yalo engasebenzisa inkundla efanayo yobuchwepheshe be-mRNA. Kungumbono osubeke amehlo awo ekubhekaneni nezifo ezivamile ezindaweni zethu njengoMalaleveva, isifo sofuba, iHIV ne-AIDS." Lomcimbi uphinde umake uhambo olubalulekile loHlelo nokwethulwa kwendawo yeHabhu yobuchwepheshe ne-mRNA e-Afrigen eKapa kukhona uDkt Tedros noDkt Matshidiso Moeti weWHO Regional Director for Afrika, Charles Gore uMqondisi oMkhulu weMPP kanye noNgqongqoshe abahlonishwayo ababamba iqhaza ekusikweni kweribhini emcimbini e-Afrigen. USolwazi Petro Terblance, uMqondisi oMkhulu we-Afrigen, uthe: "Iqembu lonke le-Afrigen liyajabula ukufika kulokhu okubalulekile nokuqedwa kwenkundla yezobuchwepheshe be-mRNA. Lenkundla inendawo ekupheleni kokuthuthukiswa komgomo mRNA nokukhiqizwa kwendawo lapho ihabhu ye-mRNA yomgomo weCOVID-19 ikhandidethi AfriVac 2121 njengamanje isezingeni lesikalo esiphezulu. Ngaphezu kwezinyanga ezingu-18 Afrigen isihambe uguquko olukhulu ngokwesekwa kohlelokuxhumana lababambisani nabeluleki abenza loluHlelo. Sesikhulise amandla ethu nokukwazi ukuhlangabezana nezinga elihle eliphezulu lokuthuthukiswa komgomo we-mRNA, ukusiza injongo yokwakha ukukwazi okugcinekile kumaLMIC ukukhiqiza imigomo ye-mRNA.". I-Afrigen isisungulwe ngempumelelo uhlelo lokukhiqizwa kwemigomo yeCOVID-19 ngesikalo selebhu njengamanje kuyanyuka uhlelo ngezinga elifanele lokukhiqiza ngezigaba imigomo ukuthi isetshenziswe ngesigaba sesibili noma sokuqala sokuhlola kwezokwelapha kumazinga eGMP.
translations.reference: mRNA Technology Transfer Programme moves to the next phase of its development Inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen, South Africa. Partners gather in Cape Town to discuss the next phase of the mRNA vaccine development and knowledge sharing. Over 200 international participants working with the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme met in Cape Town this week for their first face-to-face meeting.
